strategy: He Polymorphs at the end of round 1 into one of several animal forms, and after his first defeat he Blessings back to full health once - plan to kill him twice.

Elephant (Tim-tim): his incoming damage is capped at 100 until you hit him with the three schools he blades himself with. Bring off-school hits/treasure cards to match (Death/Life/Myth, Fire/Ice/Storm, or Balance/Myth/Ice). Progress carries between recasts; finish the code and his cap becomes a Frenzy.
Rhino (Tom-tom): he Superchargers and Stormblades himself then casts a non-dispellable Storm Owl - stack Storm shields and burst him before it lands.
Lion (Tik-tik): the first time he is damaged he Cat Naps to full health (once) - bait it with a small hit, then set up your real burst.
Spider (Tse-Tse): he stacks +30% Shadowblades each round and Sacrifices himself on round 3 - you can just survive and let him kill himself.
Otherwise hit with Myth (he takes more from Myth, resists Storm 80%) and deal with the Starburst Spider and Goliath Bruiser minions.
cheatNotes: End of round 1 he Polymorphs into one of the animal forms below. Identify which and adapt.
The first time you defeat him he casts Blessing back to full health (once). Expect to kill him twice.
As the Elephant his incoming damage is capped at 100 for the fight until you solve his code.
Round 2 and every 2 rounds he blades himself with 3 schools - the schools you must hit him with to break the cap.
Your progress through the code is not reset when he recasts the blades. Keep going.
Finishing the code replaces his damage-limit aura with Frenzy - now hit him hard.
As the Rhino he opens with two Supercharges and a +35% Stormblade on himself.
End of round 1 as the Rhino he casts a non-dispellable Storm Owl. Storm-shield the team.
As the Lion, the first damage he takes triggers a Cat Nap to full health (once). Bait it with a small hit.
As the Spider he stacks a +30% Shadowblade every round.
At the end of his 3rd round as the Spider he casts Dark Fiend and Sacrifices himself, defeating himself - you can let him do it.
